MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO WRITTEN DISCOVERY (TRANSACTION ID # 58302703) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BEAUBIEN, WILL DBA BIG NET LEASE AND BEAUBIEN INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

DISCOVERY, 302, DEFENDANT KIRK SOUTHWELL'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, AND RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND THINGS, ALL "SET NO. ONE" IS UNOPPOSED BUT DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENDANT SEEKS AN ORDER COMPELLING THE PLAINTIFF TO RESPOND TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND A REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, WITHOUT OBJECTION. IT IS STATED RESPONSES TO THE DISCOVERY WAS IN FACT SERVED, ALBEIT FIVE DAYS AFTER THEIR STATUTORY DUE DATE. A COPY OF THE PLAINTIFF'S VERIFIED RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORIES IS ATTACHED AND NO OBJECTIONS ARE STATED. PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND A REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ARE NOT ATTACHED TO THE MOVING PAPERS. THERE IS NO WAY FOR THE COURT TO KNOW IF ANY OBJECTIONS WERE STATED IN THOSE RESPONSES. UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES A MOTION TO COMPEL DIRECTED TO THE SPECIFICS OF RESPONSES RECEIVED IS APPROPRIATE. MOVING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN B. STEIN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

DECLARATION OF JAMIN P. HORN IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR JOINT ORDER (TRANSACTION ID # 60222389) FILED BY PLAINTIFF BEAUBIEN, WILL DBA BIG NET LEASE AND BEAUBIEN INVESTMENT GROUP LLC

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF WILL BEAUBIEN MOTION FOR ORDER PURSUANT TO JOINT STIPULATION OF ALL PARTIES THAT CERTAIN MATERIALS AVAILABLE ON THE INTERNET NOT BE INDEXED FOR SEARCH ENGINE PURPOSES IS DENIED. WHILE THE COURT IS SYMPATHETIC TO THE PARTIES' JOINT REQUEST, THE PROPOSED ORDER IS IN THE NATURE OF AN INJUNCTION DIRECTED TO GOOGLE AND MICROSOFT WHO ARE NOT PARTIES TO THIS CASE AND HAVE NOT BEEN PERSONALLY SERVED. WHILE IT APPEARS THAT GOOGLE AND POSSIBLY MICROSOFT MAY UNDER SOME CIRCUMSTANCES AGREE TO REMOVE SEARCH RESULTS BASED ON A COURT ORDER, NOTHING PROVIDED TO THE COURT STATES OR SUGGESTS THAT A MERE STIPULATED ORDER SUFFICES FOR THEM TO DO SO. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG; DEPT. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Pursuant To Joint Stipulation Of All Parties
Off Calendar Mar-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF WILL BEAUBIEN Motion For Order Pursuant To Joint Stipulation Of All Parties that certain materials available on the internet not be indexed for search engine purposes is denied. While the court is sympathetic to the parties' joint request, the proposed order is in the nature of an injunction directed to Google and Microsoft who are not parties to this case and have not been personally served. While it appears that Google and possibly Microsoft may under some circumstances agree to remove search results based on a court order, nothing provided to the court states or suggests that a mere stipulated order suffices for them to do so. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT KIRK SOUTHWELL'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, Mr. Southwell's demurrer to the five causes of action in the complaint is overruled. While it is certainly true than fewer than five claims would have sufficed for the simple set of facts asserted by plaintiffs, each of their five claims is adequately alleged. ORDER SIGNED. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories And Special Interrogatories, And Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Things, All "Set No. One"
Off Calendar Dec-29-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KIRK SOUTHWELL'S Motion To Compel Answers To Form Interrogatories And Special Interrogatories, And Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Things, All "Set No. One" is unopposed but denied without prejudice. Defendant seeks an Order compelling the plaintiff to respond to Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories and a Request for Production of Documents, without objection. It is stated responses to the discovery was in fact served, albeit five days after their statutory due date. A copy of the plaintiff's verified response to Form Interrogatories is attached and no objections are stated. Plaintiff's responses to Special Interrogatories and a Request for Production of Documents are not attached to the moving papers. There is no way for the Court to know if any objections were stated in those responses. Under the circumstances a motion to compel directed to the specifics of responses received is appropriate. Moving party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature. Judge Pro Tem: Steven B. Stein, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
